data.title: ISM Services PMI weakens more than expected in March
data.description:
March U.S. ISM Services PMI Index: 54.0 vs. 54.7 consensus and 56.1 in February, according to data released by the Institute for Supply Management on Monday.

Prices: 70.7 vs. 63.0 prior.

Employment: 45.2 vs. 51.8 prior.

New orders: 60.6 vs.  58.6.

Business activity/production: 53.9 vs. 59.9 in February. 

Developing… Check back for updates.

data.title: Barings BDC's private credit fund limits redemptions after rise in repurchase requests
data.description:
Barings BDC (BBDC) said its private credit fund limited redemptions after a surge in repurchase requests, consistent with broader trends.

Barings Private Credit Corporation received repurchase requests equal to 11.3% of common shares outstanding as of December 31, 2025.

The fund has accepted for repurchase 5% of the outstanding shares for the first quarter.

BBDC said it has accepted for purchase on a pro rata basis ~44.3% of the shares validly tendered and not properly withdrawn before the expiration of the offer.

data.title: Oil markets could be overstating Strait of Hormuz disruption - report
data.description:
AIS data used by markets may be underreporting as much as ~50% of actual shipping traffic in the Strait of Hormuz, which is open but regulated by Iranian authorities, Citrini Research suggested Monday in a new report.

Citrini sent a reporter to the strait to observe first-hand what is actually happening, rather than relying on data, and concluded that activity was more nuanced than conventional wisdom would indicate.

"It was clear that nobody—literally nobody, not the analysts, not the correspondents, not the retired generals doing hits on cable news and least of all us—actually had any idea what was going on," the report said. "Everyone was working from the same stale satellite imagery and the same unnamed Pentagon sources and the same AIS shipping data, which, as I would later discover, was missing roughly half of what was actually transiting the strait on any given day."

Much like Turkey manages the Bosphorus Strait connecting the Black Sea to the Sea of Marmara, Iran is treating the Strait of Hormuz as sovereign territory: Ships that pay transit fees and receive an authorization code get an escort, and those that do not just wait in the heat.

The report suggests Iran's aim is not to shut the Strait of Hormuz but to assert its sovereignty over it, having established a de facto navigation regime between Qeshm and Larak islands.

The report suggests the goal is not to shut the strait but to regulate it, and traffic is recovering but shifting, with fewer Very Large Crude Carriers and more nimble LPG ships and small tankers.

"His reaction to the idea of insurance being the only reason vessels weren't crossing was one of near incredulity," the report said. "'The bottleneck is not wanting to die. Insurance? Do you think we want to die?' he asked. 'Look, some guys will run it. Dynacom, Sinokor, they've got balls. But think about it from the owner's side. You send your ship through, it gets hit, now what? You're out a vessel in the hottest rate environment any of us have ever seen. Insurance pays you out, great, but you can't buy a replacement tomorrow. The fleet's spoken for. Meanwhile the guy who kept his ship parked as floating storage in the Gulf is printing money doing nothing. So yeah, it's not just about dying. It's about being stupid."

ETFs: (USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI)

data.title: XCana Petroleum appoints Aarya Shahsavar as President
data.description:
• XCana Petroleum (XCPT) announced on Monday that it had appointed Aarya Shahsavar as President.
  • Shahsavar brought engineering and project development experience across energy and resource sectors and had worked with the leadership team to advance operations and growth.
  • The company had aimed to advance exploration, accelerate development at its Nevada asset, strengthen partnerships, and build shareholder value.
  • XCana’s 100% owned Table Mountain project in Nevada had comprised 118 claims over 2,430 acres in a historic nickel district, with average grades of 12% nickel and 14% cobalt, targeting critical minerals for energy and supply chains.
  • The company had planned further evaluation, exploration, and development of its Nevada project to drive growth.
  • Shares -34.28%.

data.title: United Natural Foods' local union members authorize strike - update
data.description:
Updated 4/6/2026 9:44 ET with statement from United Natural Foods.

Warehouse employees at United Natural Foods’ (UNFI) Pompano Beach, Florida facility have voted unanimously to go on strike if the company does not offer a fair contract and stop its unfair labor practices.

United Natural Foods (UNFI) is the primary distributor for Amazon’s (AMZN) Whole Foods supermarkets.

The Pompano-based employees voted last year to join the powerful Teamsters union, which seeks higher wages, stronger benefits, and safer working conditions for its 5,500 members from United Natural Foods (UNFI).

"Management needs to get serious, stop breaking the law, and put a real contract on the table—or they'll be dealing with the ramifications of a strike," Local 769 President Josh Zivalich said.

“UNFI can either deliver a fair first contract, or they can face the consequences. If this company keeps stalling, Teamsters will be in the streets," added Tom Erickson, director of the Teamsters Warehouse Division. 

Source: Press Release 

The company issued the following statement to Seeking Alpha:
 
UNFI cares about its associates and has spent the past six months bargaining in good faith with the union to deliver a competitive first-time labor contract. 
 
Our proposed agreement, which includes fair wages, market-leading union healthcare, and retirement benefits, directly addresses our associates' concerns while allowing us the flexibility to continue to meet the needs of our customers and suppliers.  
 
While we are disappointed that the union has authorized a strike, we have contingency plans in place to ensure we can operate the facility and continue to service our customers. We remain committed to working with union leadership to secure a fair contract for all parties.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Monday: SLNO, MSTR, KTOS, FUBO, and more
data.description:
Stock futures exhibited mixed performance on Monday after reports that the U.S., Iran, and regional mediators are discussing terms for a potential 45-day ceasefire to end the war.

Here are some of Monday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Soleno Therapeutics (SLNO) +32% – Shares surged after Neurocrine Biosciences (NBIX) agreed to acquire Soleno Therapeutics for $53 per share in cash, valuing the deal at ~$2.9B and implying a ~34% premium to Soleno’s last close. The transaction, structured as a tender offer, will add Soleno’s recently launched rare disease drug VYKAT XR for Prader-Willi syndrome, which generated $190M in 2025 revenue with strong early adoption. Neurocrine said the deal supports its revenue growth and portfolio diversification strategy, with funding from cash on hand and modest debt. The acquisition is expected to close within ~90 days, subject to regulatory approvals. NBIX shares fell over 2% following the announcement.
  • FuboTV (FUBO) +15% - Shares surged after FuboTV issued long-term guidance pointing to a sharp profitability ramp, with pro forma adjusted EBITDA of $80M–$100M in FY2026 and a target of at least $300M by FY2028. The company expects to turn free cash flow positive in FY2027, exit FY2026 with at least $200M in cash, and reach a net cash position by FY2028, implying EBITDA growth of over 80% CAGR through the period.
  • Kratos Defense & Security Solutions (KTOS) +5% – Shares advanced after Jefferies upgraded the defense contractor to Buy from Hold, citing strong growth visibility across key segments. Analyst Sheila Kahyaoglu pointed to a potential 31% CAGR in the Government Solutions business through 2028, driven by rising hypersonic program demand. Additional upside is seen from turbine technologies tied to missile propulsion, including the Prometheus joint venture, and an expected ramp in production of the Valkyrie drone. Jefferies maintained its $85 price target, implying ~26% upside.
  • Strategy (MSTR) +4% – Shares rose after the company disclosed it purchased ~$330M worth of Bitcoin between April 1–5, adding 4,871 BTC at an average price of $67,718. The company funded the acquisition through sales of preferred stock and common shares, generating over $400M in proceeds. As of Q1-end, Strategy reported a $14.46B unrealized loss on its digital asset holdings, partially offset by a $2.42B deferred tax benefit. The firm now holds 766,970 BTC at an average purchase price of $75,644.
  • Twilio (TWLO) +3% – Shares gained after Jefferies upgraded the stock to Buy from Hold, citing stronger conviction in its role within the voice AI ecosystem. Analyst Samad Samana highlighted that increasing relevance in an agentic AI landscape, combined with improving fundamentals, could drive multiple expansion. The firm set a $160 price target, implying roughly 22% upside from current levels.
  • Netflix (NFLX) +3% – Shares rose after Goldman Sachs upgraded the stock to Buy from Neutral and raised its price target to $120, citing improved risk/reward following recent underperformance. The firm sees a return to a “standalone execution story” after Netflix abandoned its pursuit of Warner Bros. Discovery assets, unlocking focus on core growth drivers. Analysts highlighted sustained double-digit revenue growth driven by subscriber gains, pricing power, and a rapidly scaling ad business, alongside margin expansion and strong free cash flow generation. Potential for renewed share buybacks was also flagged as a key EPS tailwind.

Biggest stock losers

  • Atlas Energy Solutions (AESI) -10% - Shares plunged on announcing plans to offer $300M of convertible senior notes due 2031 in a private placement, with underwriters holding an option to purchase up to an additional $45M. Proceeds will be used in part to fund capped call transactions, repay ~$66M of obligations with Stonebriar Commercial Finance (including a termination fee), and ~$75M of borrowings under its 2023 ABL facility, with the remainder allocated to general corporate purposes. The unsecured notes will bear semi-annual interest and are callable from April 2029.
  • Carvana (CVNA) -2% - Shares slipped after Bank of America downgraded the online used-car retailer to Neutral from Buy, citing a more balanced risk/reward amid evolving macro conditions. Analyst Michael McGovern flagged concerns around an oil-driven squeeze on lower- and middle-income consumers, alongside rising 2-year rates, which could weigh on demand. The firm set a $360 price target, implying about 15% upside from current levels.

data.title: Rocket Companies, First American Financial upgraded at Barclays on mortgage volumes
data.description:
Barclays upgraded Rocket Companies (RKT) and First American Financial (FAF) on an expected increase in mortgage volumes.

"Despite a 40 bps increase in the 30Y FRM to 6.38% since the trough in February, the mortgage rate is only 23 bps higher than at the start of the year and lower than the average (6.58%) for all of FY25," said analyst Terry Ma.

"As a result, we still expect overall mortgage volumes to increase ~8.9% y/y vs. FY25," added Ma.

"Of note, our base case is for the Iran conflict to deescalate over the coming weeks. For 1Q, based on channel checks, mortgage volumes were likely robust during the first two months but softened in March, while prepayments were likely elevated through the quarter," said the research note.

RKT and FAF were raised to Overweight from Equal Weight.

"Post COOP merger, RKT has a more balanced business mix (including ~$5B of servicing revenues) that should help the company generate more stable operating results through the interest rate cycle. We think the recent sell-off underappreciates this point," said the research note.

"Similar to RKT, we view the valuation for FAF as overly discounted," said Ma.

data.title: Only three energy large cap stocks have A-grade EPS revisions
data.description:
As Q1 2026 earnings season is set to kick off, investors are scrutinizing the energy sector against a backdrop of volatile crude prices, shifting demand dynamics, and ongoing geopolitical uncertainty.

While the small-cap energy space has produced a healthy cohort of stocks with strong upward EPS revisions, the picture looks strikingly different among large-caps, where only three names have managed to earn a Seeking Alpha EPS Revision Quant Grade of A- or higher.

Leading the group is Texas Pacific Land Corporation (TPL), the only large-cap energy stock to earn the highest possible A+ grade, reflecting strong and consistent upward revisions from analysts covering the oil and gas exploration and production space.

Valero Energy Corporation (VLO) and Viper Energy, Inc. (VNOM) round out the trio, each earning A- grades, a signal that analysts have been cautiously but meaningfully raising their earnings expectations ahead of Q1 results.

  1. Texas Pacific Land Corporation (TPL) - EPS Revision Grade: A+
  2. Valero Energy Corporation (VLO) - EPS Revision Grade: A-
  3. Viper Energy, Inc. (VNOM) - EPS Revision Grade: A-

Energy ETFs: (XLE), (AMLP), (VDE), (XOP), (OIH), and (IXC)

data.title: Most and least shorted stocks over $2B market cap
data.description:
Short interest across stocks with market caps above $2B highlights a sharp divide, with bearish positioning concentrated in high-beta tech, healthcare, and consumer names, while large-cap, stable cash-flow businesses continue to see minimal short activity.

Among the most shorted stocks are Hims & Hers Health (HIMS) at 37.93%, SoundHound AI (SOUN) at 35.04%, and Ondas Holdings (ONDS) at 33.23%. On the other end, the least shorted stocks include names like Alphabet (GOOG) and Mastercard (MA).

Most shorted stocks (market cap above $2B):

  • Hims & Hers Health (HIMS): 37.93%
  • SoundHound AI (SOUN): 35.04%
  • Ondas Holdings (ONDS): 33.23%
  • Tango Therapeutics (TNGX): 33.11%
  • CleanSpark (CLSK): 33.08%
  • MARA Holdings (MARA): 30.08%
  • Upstart Holdings (UPST): 29.27%
  • Applied Digital (APLD): 28.17%
  • Acadia Healthcare (ACHC): 27.23%
  • RH (RH): 25.72%

Least shorted stocks (market cap above $2B):

  • Rush Enterprises (RUSHA): 0.57%
  • Ubiquiti (UI): 0.61%
  • SOLV Energy (MWH): 0.63%
  • Alphabet (GOOG): 0.64%
  • Waste Connections (WCN): 0.69%
  • Kyivstar Group (KYIV): 0.70%
  • Mastercard (MA): 0.71%
  • Adams Diversified Equity Fund (ADX): 0.72%
  • CNA Financial (CNA): 0.73%
  • RBC Bearings (RBC): 0.74%

Broad-market ETFs such as (SPY), (QQQ), and (IWM) track some of these names.

data.title: Canada S&P Global Services PMI eases in March, climbs to 47.2
data.description: The S&P Global Canada Services PMI Business Activity Index in March was at 47.2, an increase from 46.5 in February, but still shows a decline in activity, especially in business services and transport & storage.
